title: When I have free time I prefer to:

options.text: Spend time with friends or family
options.example: Going to parties, meeting people, or hanging out with groups

options.text: Spend time alone or with a few close people
options.example: Reading, watching movies, or having deep conversations with close friends
I'm comfortable expressing my thoughts in front of others
I prefer to understand what others think before sharing my opinion

title: After a busy day I feel more refreshed by:

options.example: Socializing helps me recharge and feel energized
I need solitude to process the day and restore my energy
I enjoy contributing to discussions and sharing my thoughts
I prefer to listen and reflect before contributing

title: When meeting new people I usually:

options.example: I'm comfortable initiating conversations with strangers

options.text: Wait for others to start talking
options.example: I prefer when others make the first move in conversations

title: When solving a problem I prefer:

options.example: Discussing helps me think through solutions better
I work best when I can think through problems independently

title: Group work for me is usually:

options.example: I find energy and inspiration working with others
I can work in groups but it drains my energy
I like to express my thoughts verbally and immediately
I prefer to organize my thoughts before sharing them

title: When learning something new I prefer:

options.example: I learn best with concrete examples and hands-on experience
I enjoy exploring concepts and theoretical possibilities

title: In studies I enjoy subjects that:

options.example: Science, history, or subjects with clear factual content
Philosophy, literature, or subjects that explore possibilities

title: When solving a problem I prefer:

options.example: I trust established procedures and tested solutions
I like to experiment with innovative solutions
What is happening now and concrete information
What could happen and potential opportunities
Step-by-step instructions and concrete details
Theoretical frameworks and abstract concepts
I work best with detailed guidelines and specifications
I like to experiment and try various approaches

options.text: Are useful in real life immediately
options.example: Practical skills I can apply right away
Theoretical knowledge that expands my understanding
Clear, sequential learning with detailed guidance
Grasping the big picture before diving into details

title: When making decisions I usually rely on:

options.example: I analyze data and use rational thinking
I consider what feels right and aligns with my values

title: If two people disagree I usually:

options.text: Focus on the most logical solution
options.example: I look for the objectively best answer
I try to understand both perspectives and emotions
I give straightforward, factual feedback
I focus on being encouraging and considerate

title: When solving conflicts I focus more on:

options.example: Finding the most rational and just solution
Keeping relationships positive and peaceful
Well-supported logical points and evidence
Empathy and considering different viewpoints
Looking at facts and logical merit
Thinking about how it affects individuals and relationships

title: When rules are broken I usually focus on:

options.example: Applying rules consistently and justly

options.text: Why the person behaved that way
options.example: Understanding the circumstances and motivations

title: When choosing between options I usually:

options.example: I weigh pros and cons objectively
I think about how each choice will make me and others feel

title: When starting a project I prefer:

options.example: I like to have a detailed plan before beginning

options.text: Starting and adjusting along the way
options.example: I prefer to be flexible and adapt as I go
I like having structure and knowing what to expect
I prefer keeping my options open and being spontaneous
I like to complete work well before deadlines
I often do my best work under time pressure
I like to know what I'm doing and when
I prefer to keep my options open and decide spontaneously
I follow a regular study routine and timetable
I study when I feel motivated or inspired
I like to research and plan activities beforehand
I enjoy discovering things spontaneously
I like closure and having matters settled
I prefer keeping possibilities available

title: When working on assignments I prefer:

options.example: I work systematically through each part
I work intensively when I feel motivated
